saleable.explanation_en: easy to sell; attractive to customers
saleable.example_sentence: We need to make our new design more saleable to younger customers.
saleable.original_sentence: Listening to people provides definite ideas for making the product more saleable.

manufacturer.explanation_en: a person or company that makes goods for sale
manufacturer.example_sentence: The car manufacturer is releasing a new electric model next year.
manufacturer.original_sentence: It also suggests to the manufacturer what he should tell consumers about the product in his advertising.

see things through someone's eyes.explanation_en: to understand a situation from another person's perspective
see things through someone's eyes.example_sentence: To be a good teacher, you must try to see things through your students' eyes.
see things through someone's eyes.original_sentence: To sell John Brown what John Brown buys, you've got to see things through John Brown's eyes.

raw materials.explanation_en: basic substances or information used to create something new
raw materials.example_sentence: These interviews provided the raw materials for his new book.
raw materials.original_sentence: They feed your mind raw materials that can be converted into creative power.

draw out.explanation_en: to encourage someone to talk or express their feelings
draw out.example_sentence: The interviewer tried to draw out the shy candidate by asking about his hobbies.
draw out.original_sentence: In personal conversation or in group meetings, draw out people with little urges.

soak up.explanation_en: to absorb or learn information easily
soak up.example_sentence: Children soak up new languages much faster than adults.
soak up.original_sentence: Your mind soaks up raw material that you can use to produce creative thought, and you win friends.

smooth and polish.explanation_en: to improve something by making small changes or refinements
smooth and polish.example_sentence: We spent the afternoon smoothing and polishing our presentation before the meeting.
smooth and polish.original_sentence: Let other people help you smooth and polish your ideas.

dogmatic.explanation_en: being very certain that your opinions are right and others are wrong
dogmatic.example_sentence: It is difficult to have a discussion with him because he is so dogmatic about his political views.

chances are.example_sentence: Chances are you will be tired after the long flight.
chances are.original_sentence: If you do, chances are you'll end up with a better idea.

end up with.explanation_en: to finally have or reach a particular result
end up with.example_sentence: If you don't plan your project, you might end up with a poor result.
end up with.original_sentence: If you do, chances are you'll end up with a better idea.

penetrate.explanation_en: to go into or pass through something; to fully understand
penetrate.example_sentence: It took a while for the news to penetrate his mind.
penetrate.original_sentence: Listening means letting what's said penetrate your mind.

take over.explanation_en: to take control or start doing something that someone else was doing
take over.example_sentence: She waited for him to stop talking so she could take over the conversation.
take over.original_sentence: They're just waiting for the other person to pause so they can take over with the talking.
